methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 36.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Viridian Space is a Pre-Seed stage company in Space & Aerospace founded by Rostislav Spektor and Matthew Feldman, with $2M+ in total capital raised. The sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 identifiable private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: Stage-Progression. Using the $2M total raised as a proxy for enterprise value, a typical Pre-Seed to next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $5M. This method assumes Viridian's subsequent funding round will follow standard progression patterns observed across early-stage capital allocation. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target; while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 36.9x P/S across the public universe, this metric is not actionable without private revenue figures. Peer-Median triangulation is inapplicable: although 25 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three meet adjacent-stage or similar-tier filters, insufficient for statistical reliability.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $4M–$6M, with a median estimate of $5M. Key uncertainties include the company's actual revenue run-rate, which would unlock public-comp validation, and the pace of product development relative to fundraising runway. The narrow method base reflects typical Pre-Seed information constraints and underscores the importance of revenue disclosure in subsequent funding rounds.
